---
name: expense-logger
description: Track and categorize personal expenses with monthly summaries, pie charts, filtering, and CSV export
metadata:
  contentType: finance
  structure: collection
  boundary: none
  scale: "2"
---

# Expense Logger

A personal expense tracking module that helps you record, categorize, and analyze your spending.

## Features

- **Record expenses** with amount, category, date, and optional notes
- **Categories**: Food, Transport, Housing, Entertainment, Utilities, Other
- **Monthly summary** with category breakdowns displayed as a pie chart
- **Filter** expenses by date range and/or category
- **Export** expense data as CSV for use in spreadsheets

## Usage

### Adding an Expense

Submit an expense with a required amount, category, and date. Notes are optional.

### Viewing Expenses

Browse all expenses or filter by:
- **Date range**: specify start and/or end dates
- **Category**: filter to a single category

### Monthly Summary

View a summary for any month showing:
- Total spending
- Per-category totals, counts, and percentages
- SVG pie chart visualization of category breakdown

### CSV Export

Export filtered or unfiltered expense data as a downloadable CSV file with columns: date, category, amount, notes.

- **Monthly summary** page with total spending, per-category breakdowns (count, total, percentage), and an SVG pie chart with legend
- **CSV export** with optional filtering — downloads as `expenses-YYYY-MM-DD.csv`
- **Delete** individual expenses
- **JSON API** endpoints at `/api/expenses` and `/api/summary` for programmatic access

Support adding markers with title and description popups, drawing circles to highlight areas, and a location search bar. Include a sidebar listing all markers with click-to-center functionality. Support marker categories with color-coded pins.
hint: Use leaflet package. Initialize map with L.map(). Add tile layer from OpenStreetMap. Markers via L.marker([lat, lng]).bindPopup(). Circles via L.circle(). Geocoding search can use Nominatim API format. Category colors via custom icon with different-colored markers. MSS: contentType geo, structure collection, mechanics filter, boundary ask, scale 3.

The module is complete with 8 files:

- **`package.json`** — Module metadata with `modnet` MSS tags (`geo` / `form` / `filter,track` / `ask` / scale 2)
- **`types.ts`** — `MapMarker`, `MapCircle`, `MarkerCategory` types plus category color/label maps
- **`server.ts`** — Bun HTTP server with REST API for markers and circles CRUD, serves HTML and client JS
- **`render.ts`** — SSR page rendering using plaited `h()` with full CSS and HTML structure (sidebar, map container, modals)
- **`client.js`** — Browser-side Leaflet map logic handling all interactivity
- **`data/markers.json`** / **`data/circles.json`** — Persistent JSON storage (start empty)
- **`skills/interactive-map/SKILL.md`** — Skill definition with frontmatter

### Features implemented:
- **Leaflet map** with OpenStreetMap tiles
- **Add markers** — click "Add Marker" button, click map, fill form with title/description/category
- **Color-coded categories** — 6 categories (Default, Restaurant, Park, Shop, Landmark, Custom) with distinct SVG pin colors
- **Draw circles** — click "Draw Circle", click map for center, enter radius and pick color
- **Location search** — search bar using Nominatim geocoding, flies to result
- **Sidebar** — lists all markers with color dots, click-to-center + open popup, delete button
- **Category filters** — checkbox toggles to show/hide markers by category
- **XSS protection** — HTML escaping on all user-provided content in popups and sidebar

---
name: interactive-map
description: Interactive Leaflet map with markers, circles, search, and category filtering
metadata:
  contentType: geo
  structure: form
  boundary: ask
  scale: "2"
---

# Interactive Map

An interactive map module built on Leaflet.js with OpenStreetMap tiles. Provides a full-featured mapping experience with marker management, area highlighting, and location search.

## Features

- **Map View**: Pan and zoom an OpenStreetMap-based map
- **Markers**: Add markers with title, description, and category. Color-coded pins distinguish categories (Default, Restaurant, Park, Shop, Landmark, Custom)
- **Circles**: Draw circles to highlight geographic areas with custom radius and color
- **Location Search**: Search bar powered by Nominatim geocoding to fly to any location
- **Sidebar**: Lists all markers with click-to-center functionality and delete controls
- **Category Filters**: Toggle marker visibility by category using checkbox filters

## Usage

1. Start the server — the map loads centered on a default location
2. Click **Add Marker**, then click the map to place a marker. Fill in title, description, and category
3. Click **Draw Circle**, then click the map to set center. Enter radius and pick a color
4. Use the **search bar** to find and fly to locations worldwide
5. Use **category checkboxes** in the sidebar to filter which markers are visible
6. Click any marker in the **sidebar list** to center the map on it

## API Endpoints

- `GET /` — Serve the map UI
- `GET /api/markers` — List all markers
- `POST /api/markers` — Create a marker
- `DELETE /api/markers/:id` — Remove a marker
- `GET /api/circles` — List all circles
- `POST /api/circles` — Create a circle
- `DELETE /api/circles/:id` — Remove a circle

- **`package.json`** — Module metadata with `modnet` MSS tags (`geo` / `form` / `filter,track` / `ask` / scale 2)
- **`types.ts`** — `MapMarker`, `MapCircle`, `MarkerCategory` types plus category color/label maps
- **`server.ts`** — Bun HTTP server with REST API for markers and circles CRUD, serves HTML and client JS
- **`render.ts`** — SSR page rendering using plaited `h()` with full CSS and HTML structure (sidebar, map container, modals)
- **`client.js`** — Browser-side Leaflet map logic handling all interactivity
- **`data/markers.json`** / **`data/circles.json`** — Persistent JSON storage (start empty)
- **`skills/interactive-map/SKILL.md`** — Skill definition with frontmatter

### Features implemented:
- **Leaflet map** with OpenStreetMap tiles
- **Add markers** — click "Add Marker" button, click map, fill form with title/description/category
- **Color-coded categories** — 6 categories (Default, Restaurant, Park, Shop, Landmark, Custom) with distinct SVG pin colors
- **Draw circles** — click "Draw Circle", click map for center, enter radius and pick color
- **Location search** — search bar using Nominatim geocoding, flies to result
- **Sidebar** — lists all markers with color dots, click-to-center + open popup, delete button
- **Category filters** — checkbox toggles to show/hide markers by category
- **XSS protection** — HTML escaping on all user-provided content in popups and sidebar

---
name: habit-tracker
description: Track daily and weekly habits with streak views and completion rates
metadata:
  contentType: health
  structure: steps
  boundary: none
  scale: "2"
---

# Habit Tracker

A personal habit tracking module that helps users build and maintain daily and weekly habits.

## Features

- **Define habits** with a name and target frequency (daily or weekly)
- **Today's checklist** showing all habits with toggleable completion status
- **Weekly streak view** displaying a 7-day grid of completions per habit
- **Completion rate** percentage for the current week
- **Persistent storage** of habit definitions and completion records

## Views

The module has two main views navigated via tabs:

1. **Today** — A checklist of all habits for today. Tap to mark complete/incomplete.
2. **Week** — A 7-day streak grid (Mon–Sun) showing completion dots per habit, plus an overall completion rate percentage.

## Data

- `data/habits.json` — Array of habit definitions (`id`, `name`, `frequency`, `createdAt`)
- `data/completions.json` — Array of completion records (`habitId`, `date`)

## Usage

- Add a new habit by providing a name and selecting daily or weekly frequency
- Check off habits as you complete them throughout the day
- Switch to the weekly view to see your streaks and overall completion rate
- Remove habits you no longer want to track

trajectory.content: The module is complete. Let me verify the file structure:

The habit tracker module is ready. Here's what was created:

**Files:**
- `package.json` — Module config with MSS tags (`health`, `steps`, `track`, `none`, scale 2)
- `skills/habit-tracker/SKILL.md` — Seed skill documentation
- `types.ts` — Type definitions for `Habit`, `Completion`, `Frequency`, and view data types
- `store.ts` — Data persistence layer using `Bun.file`/`Bun.write` for `data/habits.json` and `data/completions.json`, plus helpers for week date math and completion rate calculation
- `render.ts` — UI rendering with plaited `h()` templates, producing styled HTML for both views
- `index.ts` — Bun HTTP server with routes for the two views and form actions
- `data/habits.json` & `data/completions.json` — Empty initial data files

**Features:**
- **Today tab** (`/`) — Add habits with name + frequency (daily/weekly), toggle completion checkboxes, delete habits
- **Week tab** (`/week`) — 7-day Mon–Sun streak grid with colored dots per habit, plus a completion rate percentage card
- All state persists to JSON files in `data/`

input: Create a crypto portfolio tracker module. Let users add coin holdings with symbol, amount held, and purchase price. Show current portfolio value using stored price data, gain/loss per coin as a percentage, and an allocation pie chart. Support manual price updates for each coin. Persist holdings and prices in the module's data directory.
hint: Store holdings in data/holdings.json and prices in data/prices.json (user-updatable). Show P/L per coin and total portfolio value. SVG pie chart for allocation by value. MSS: contentType finance, structure collection, boundary none, scale 2.

trials.output:
The module is complete. Here's what was created:

**Files:**
- `package.json` — Module manifest with `finance` contentType, `collection` structure, `none` boundary
- `skills/crypto-tracker/SKILL.md` — Seed skill with endpoint docs
- `types.ts` — Type definitions for holdings, prices, and portfolio metrics
- `data.ts` — Persistence layer using `Bun.file`/`Bun.write` for JSON storage
- `portfolio.ts` — Computes per-coin metrics (gain/loss %, current value) and aggregates allocations
- `chart.ts` — SVG pie chart generator with legend (no external deps)
- `render.ts` — Full HTML dashboard with dark theme, summary cards, holdings table, add form, and chart
- `server.ts` — Bun HTTP server with REST API + form-based routes
- `data/holdings.json` / `data/prices.json` — Empty initial data files

---
name: crypto-tracker
description: Track crypto holdings with gain/loss and allocation pie chart
metadata:
  contentType: finance
  structure: collection
  boundary: none
  scale: "2"
---

# Crypto Portfolio Tracker

Track your cryptocurrency portfolio with real-time gain/loss calculations and visual allocation breakdowns.

## Features

- **Add Holdings**: Record coin symbol, amount held, and purchase price
- **Portfolio Overview**: View total portfolio value, per-coin gain/loss percentages
- **Allocation Chart**: SVG pie chart showing portfolio allocation by coin
- **Manual Price Updates**: Update current market prices for each coin
- **Persistence**: All holdings and prices stored in JSON files

## Usage

### Endpoints

- `GET /` — Portfolio dashboard with holdings table and allocation pie chart
- `GET /api/holdings` — JSON list of all holdings
- `POST /api/holdings` — Add a new holding `{ symbol, amount, purchasePrice }`
- `DELETE /api/holdings/:id` — Remove a holding by ID
- `PUT /api/holdings/:id` — Update a holding `{ symbol?, amount?, purchasePrice? }`
- `GET /api/prices` — JSON map of current prices
- `PUT /api/prices/:symbol` — Update current price `{ price }`

### Data Format

Holdings are stored in `data/holdings.json` and prices in `data/prices.json`.

import { h } from '@plaited/temple'; import type { PortfolioSummary } from './types'; import { renderPieChart } from './chart'; const fmt = (n: number): string => n.toLocaleString('en-US', { minimumFractionDigits: 2, maximumFractionDigits: 2 }); const fmtPct = (n: number): string => `${n >= 0 ? '+' : ''}${n.toFixed(2)}%`; const styles = { page: 'background:#1a1a2e;color:#eee;font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,sans-serif;min-height:100vh;padding:24px;margin:0;', header: 'display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:center;margin-bottom:24px;', title: 'font-size:24px;font-weight:700;color:#fff;', summaryRow: 'display:flex;gap:16px;margin-bottom:24px;flex-wrap:wrap;', card: 'background:#2a2a3e;border-radius:12px;padding:16px 20px;flex:1;min-width:160px;', cardLabel: 'font-size:12px;color:#888;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:1px;margin-bottom:4px;', cardValue: 'font-size:22px;font-weight:600;', section: 'background:#2a2a3e;border-radius:12px;padding:20px;margin-bottom:24px;', sectionTitle: 'font-size:16px;font-weight:600;margin-bottom:16px;color:#fff;', table: 'width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:14px;', th: 'text-align:left;padding:10px 12px;border-bottom:1px solid #3a3a4e;color:#888;font-weight:500;font-size:12px;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.5px;', td: 'padding:10px 12px;border-bottom:1px solid #2a2a3e;', positive: 'color:#00d395;', negative: 'color:#e84142;', neutral: 'color:#888;', form: 'display:flex;gap:10px;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:flex-end;', inputGroup: 'display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:4px;', label: 'font-size:11px;color:#888;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.5px;', input: 'background:#1a1a2e;border:1px solid #3a3a4e;border-radius:6px;padding:8px 12px;color:#eee;font-size:14px;width:120px;', btn: 'background:#627eea;color:#fff;border:none;border-radius:6px;padding:8px 16px;cursor:pointer;font-size:14px;font-weight:500;', btnDanger: 'background:#e84142;color:#fff;border:none;border-radius:6px;padding:4px 10px;cursor:pointer;font-size:12px;', btnSmall: 'background:#3a3a4e;color:#ccc;border:none;border-radius:4px;padding:4px 8px;cursor:pointer;font-size:12px;', chartWrap: 'display:flex;justify-content:center;padding:10px 0;', }; const gainLossStyle = (value: number): string => value > 0 ? styles.positive : value < 0 ? styles.negative : styles.neutral; export const renderDashboard = (summary: PortfolioSummary): string => { const holdingsRows = summary.holdings.map( (h_item) => ` ${h_item.symbol} ${fmt(h_item.amount)} $${fmt(h_item.purchasePrice)} $${fmt(h_item.currentPrice)}
$${fmt(h_item.currentValue)} $${fmt(h_item.costBasis)} $${fmt(h_item.gainLoss)} (${fmtPct(h_item.gainLossPercent)})
` ); const pieChart = renderPieChart(summary.allocations); return ` Crypto Portfolio Tracker
Crypto Portfolio
Total Value
$${fmt(summary.totalValue)}
Total Cost
$${fmt(summary.totalCost)}
Total Gain/Loss
$${fmt(summary.totalGainLoss)} (${fmtPct(summary.totalGainLossPercent)})
Holdings
${summary.holdings.length}
Add Holding
Holdings
${ summary.holdings.length === 0 ? '

No holdings yet. Add your first coin above.

' : `${holdingsRows.join('')}
Coin Amount Buy Price Current Price Value Cost Basis Gain/Loss
` }
Allocation
${pieChart}
`; };

---
name: code-snippets
description: Save, browse, and filter code snippets by language and tag
metadata:
  contentType: tools
  structure: collection
  boundary: none
  scale: "2"
---

# Code Snippets Manager

A personal code snippet manager for saving and organizing reusable code fragments.

## Features

- **Save snippets** with a title, programming language, tags, and code content
- **Browse** all saved snippets in a filterable collection view
- **Filter** by programming language or tag to find snippets quickly
- **Copy to clipboard** with a single click on any snippet
- **Persistent storage** — snippets are saved to disk as JSON

## Usage

- `GET /` — Renders the snippet collection with optional query filters (`?lang=` and `?tag=`)
- `GET /snippet/:id` — Renders a single snippet detail view
- `POST /snippets` — Create a new snippet (fields: title, language, tags, code)
- `DELETE /snippets/:id` — Remove a snippet by ID

input: Create a quiz builder module. Let users create quizzes with a title and multiple-choice questions (question text, 4 answer options, one marked correct). Support a take-quiz flow: show questions one at a time with a next button, then a final score screen showing how many were correct with a per-question breakdown of correct/incorrect. Persist quiz definitions in the module's data directory.
hint: Two modes: build (create quiz + add questions) and take (step-by-step flow). MUST create data/ directory and write quiz definitions to data/quizzes.json using Bun.write. Steps structure: one question per step, final score step. MSS: contentType education, structure steps, boundary ask (quizzes are shared with participants), scale 2.

All files are created. Here's the module structure:

```
quiz-builder/
├── package.json          # modnet metadata (education/steps/track/ask/2)
├── index.ts              # Bun.serve HTTP server with routing
├── render.ts             # Page templates using plaited h() + createSSR()
├── store.ts              # CRUD operations on data/quizzes.json
├── types.ts              # Quiz & Question types
├── data/
│   └── quizzes.json      # Persistent quiz storage (starts empty)
└── skills/
    └── quiz-builder/
        └── SKILL.md      # Seed skill definition
```

**Flows:**

- **Home** (`/`) — Lists all quizzes with Take, Edit, Delete actions
- **Create** (`/create`) — Enter a quiz title, then redirected to the editor
- **Edit** (`/edit/:id`) — View existing questions, add new ones (question text + 4 options + correct answer selector), remove questions
- **Take** (`/take/:id?q=N&a=...`) — One question at a time with radio buttons, progress bar, and Next/Finish button. Answers accumulate stateless via query params.
- **Results** (`/results/:id?a=...`) — Shows score (e.g. 3/5, 60%), plus per-question breakdown with green checkmarks for correct and red X with the correct answer shown for incorrect. Retake button included.

input.content:
---
name: quiz-builder
description: Create and take multiple-choice quizzes with scoring
metadata:
  contentType: education
  structure: steps
  boundary: ask
  scale: "2"
---

# Quiz Builder

Create multiple-choice quizzes and take them with instant scoring.

## Features

- **Create quizzes** — Set a title and add multiple-choice questions (4 options each, one correct).
- **Edit quizzes** — Add questions one at a time to a quiz.
- **Take quizzes** — Step through questions one at a time, then see a final score.
- **Score breakdown** — See total score and per-question correct/incorrect details.

## Usage

1. Navigate to the home page to see all quizzes.
2. Click **New Quiz** to create a quiz with a title.
3. Add questions with four answer options, marking one as correct.
4. Click **Take Quiz** to go through questions one by one.
5. After the last question, view your score and per-question breakdown.

## Data

Quiz definitions are persisted as JSON in the `data/quizzes.json` file.

trajectory.content: All files have been created. Let me verify the structure is correct.

All files are created. Here's the module structure:

```
quiz-builder/
├── package.json          # modnet metadata (education/steps/track/ask/2)
├── index.ts              # Bun.serve HTTP server with routing
├── render.ts             # Page templates using plaited h() + createSSR()
├── store.ts              # CRUD operations on data/quizzes.json
├── types.ts              # Quiz & Question types
├── data/
│   └── quizzes.json      # Persistent quiz storage (starts empty)
└── skills/
    └── quiz-builder/
        └── SKILL.md      # Seed skill definition
```

**Flows:**

- **Home** (`/`) — Lists all quizzes with Take, Edit, Delete actions
- **Create** (`/create`) — Enter a quiz title, then redirected to the editor
- **Edit** (`/edit/:id`) — View existing questions, add new ones (question text + 4 options + correct answer selector), remove questions
- **Take** (`/take/:id?q=N&a=...`) — One question at a time with radio buttons, progress bar, and Next/Finish button. Answers accumulate stateless via query params.
- **Results** (`/results/:id?a=...`) — Shows score (e.g. 3/5, 60%), plus per-question breakdown with green checkmarks for correct and red X with the correct answer shown for incorrect. Retake button included.
